source.pageName: Wiki class template components and notes

itemExplanationModal.rule: Use this to locate the modal overlay pattern. It is only for source pages where an item explanation is actually shown in a modal.

documentGuideDetail.rule: Use these aliases to quickly locate the document/guide article template family. These pages are article-like guide details, not normal structured item detail pages.

source.notes: Template contract derived from the Wiki design-system page. Keep this file separate from the map template contract.

defaultCanvas.rule: Use 1000 x 610 as the default generated Wiki canvas across Wiki template outputs.

observedFigmaViewport.rule: Home, SecondaryPage, SecondaryPageLargeCard, SecondaryPageMultiNav, and DetailPageLargeCardExpanded are 1000 x 610 snapshots. DetailPageAllStates is a 1000 x 1166 full-stack reference only. Generated output should keep the default 1000 x 610 canvas unless a host shell explicitly overrides it and should use vertical scrolling for overflow content.

desktop.body: x=0, y=80 or 84, w=1000, h=526 or 530 depending on state
desktop.contentInset: home uses x=32, list/detail use x=20 or x=32 according to observed state
desktop.scrollbar: right edge visual scroll component; home x=988, list/detail x=980 or x=988 depending on content state

mobile.canvas: 375 x 812 Figma reference; implementation fills the actual host container or browser viewport width below 600px instead of hardcoding 375px
mobile.header: MobileHeader x=0, y=0, w=375, h=92 is reference chrome only; production header width is 100% of the actual mobile page container, not a formal shared component in this contract pass. When the host/titlebar is outside the Wiki surface, the dark Wiki shell starts with an internal h=68 action bar using 12px inset and 44x44 search/menu icon buttons.
mobile.body: content starts at y=104 with 12px left/right inset; 351px is the 375px reference content width, while production content width is calc(100% - 24px)
mobile.fontFamily: PingFang SC, Microsoft YaHei UI, sans-serif
mobile.primaryNavMenu: Right header menu opens node 590:9324 and reuses desktop Nav.TopBar datasource/active state as first-level Wiki navigation. In the embedded dark Wiki shell, open it below the internal 68px action bar and render first-level items as min-height 56px touch rows inside the 12px-inset content column.
mobile.navigation: Secondary tabs and mobile local Nav rows scroll horizontally and do not wrap.
mobile.scrollbars: Do not show native scrollbars or Layout.Scroll visual scrollbar chrome on mobile; preserve touch scrolling when content overflows.
mobile.hover: Do not render hover visual states on mobile. Ignore :hover, state=hover, --hover, and --state-hover presentation, including Base.ToolTip hover bubbles, while preserving selected, active, focus-visible, pressed, and open states.
mobile.documentSideModules: Desktop document left anchors and right related-info sidebars are not rendered on mobile.
mobile.videoModal: Mobile video modal remains TODO_FROM_FIGMA_OR_IMPLEMENTATION until a Figma mobile video state exists.

templateUsagePolicy.rule: Wiki Figma template pages are placement and spacing references, not mandatory datasource shapes. A generator should choose regions and components from the original source page structure first, then apply the matching layout coordinates from the closest template reference.
templateUsagePolicy.componentSelection: Choose the component family that best represents the source entry information density and hierarchy. Do not force all entries into the card family shown in the selected template page.
templateUsagePolicy.partialTemplateUse: It is valid to use only a subset of a template page, such as only the secondary tab row, only the large-card grid, or only a detail section stack, when the rest of the source page does not contain matching structure.
templateUsagePolicy.noFabrication: Never fabricate second-level tabs, third-level sidebars, breadcrumbs, images, badges, counts, progress, pagination, tables, or detail sections solely because a reference template includes them.
templateUsagePolicy.layoutBinding: After a component family is selected, use the corresponding observed template layout for that component family: ShowCard lists use ShowCard list coordinates, DescribeCard rich lists use DescribeCard coordinates, and large image-first lists use SecondaryPageLargeCard coordinates.
templateUsagePolicy.plainSecondaryListTopOffset: When a secondary page has no second-level navigation row, no third-level sidebar/navigation, and no breadcrumbs above the list, place the list region 20px from the Body top. Do not reuse the lower y position reserved for pages that include breadcrumbs or secondary navigation.
templateUsagePolicy.breadcrumbPlacement: When Nav.Breadcrumbs is rendered, place it in the first row of the Body at bodyRelativeX=32, bodyRelativeY=16, height=26. Structured detail pages use width=294 and content starts at bodyRelativeY=62. Document guide detail pages use width=188 and ArticleLayout starts at bodyRelativeY=62. Breadcrumbs are not rendered on secondary pages unless a future routing contract explicitly enables them.
templateUsagePolicy.cardLikeNoThirdLevelGrid: For card-like large Layout.DescribeCard grids without third-level navigation, use repeat(4, 210px) by default. If the available content width cannot fit four 210px columns plus gaps, downgrade to repeat(3, 210px). A single rendered row is vertically centered after the column count is chosen; item count or single-row alignment must not reduce the column count by itself.
templateUsagePolicy.pageScopedCardSetConsistency: Card family, image frame ratio, and similar card-set consistency rules are scoped to the current rendered page/list only: the active secondary tab plus optional active third-level group. Do not inherit a component family or imageRatio from sibling second-level tabs, sibling secondary pages, or the whole active first-level Nav.TopBar category.
templateUsagePolicy.documentGuideDetail: Use guide.document-detail for article-like guide pages with long prose, one main media block, article metadata, optional section anchors, and optional related-info links. Choose among GuideDetailWithNavAndInfo, GuideDetailWithInfo, and GuideDetailWithNav based on the source data; do not force nav or related info when the source lacks those structures.
templateUsagePolicy.itemExplanationModal: Use modal.item-explanation only as an overlay state above the current page when the original source page opens a modal to explain a single item. Do not replace normal detail pages, tooltips, card descriptions, or map popups with Modal just because the item has explanatory text.
templateUsagePolicy.mobileBreakpoint: When rendered page width is less than 600px, use the mobile Wiki template family. When width is 600px or greater, use the desktop template family.
templateUsagePolicy.mobileResponsiveWidth: Below 600px, the Wiki page root/frame must fill the available host container or browser viewport width. Treat 375px as the Figma reference width only; do not hardcode production page, body, content, list, or card widths to 375/351/335/319px. Convert reference widths to 100% of the relevant mobile container or calc(100% - observed insets).
templateUsagePolicy.mobileNoScrollbars: Below 600px, all Wiki pages hide scrollbar chrome. Real content scrolling remains enabled where needed, but native scrollbars and Layout.Scroll visual scrollbar instances are not displayed.
templateUsagePolicy.mobileNoHover: Below 600px, all Wiki pages suppress hover visual states, including CSS :hover, explicit state=hover / --hover / --state-hover classes, and Base.ToolTip hover bubbles. Do not suppress selected, active, focus-visible, pressed, or open states, and do not rely on hover-only tooltip disclosure for required mobile information.
templateUsagePolicy.mobileAdaptationPriority: Below 600px, preserve the host page's already selected or product-customized source structure, component family, field order, optional detail visibility, and real data first. Use the mobile template nodes as layout-method references for stacking, widths, typography, horizontal scrolling, image sizing, and side-module visibility. Do not replace a customized page with a full observed mobile sample page, and do not fabricate, reorder, or remove business details solely to match the reference screenshot.
templateUsagePolicy.mobileHeaderPolicy: MobileHeader and TitlebarCenter are template chrome for mobile Wiki pages in this contract pass. Do not register a new Nav.MobileHeader component yet.
templateUsagePolicy.mobileInternalActionBar: When the host/product titlebar is outside the Wiki surface, the dark Wiki shell owns a 68px internal action bar: padding 12px, left search icon button 44x44, right primary-menu icon button 44x44, desktop Nav.TopBar hidden, and desktop Form.SearchBar input hidden.
templateUsagePolicy.mobilePrimaryNavMenu: The right header menu opens the mobile primary navigation panel from node 590:9324 and reuses the desktop Nav.TopBar item datasource and active first-level category state.
templateUsagePolicy.mobilePrimaryNavMenuGeometry: In the embedded dark Wiki shell, the primary nav panel opens at top=68 below the internal action bar, covers the remaining shell, uses 12px side inset / 351px reference content column, and renders each item as a min-height 56px touch row with the active brand gradient/underline treatment.
templateUsagePolicy.mobileDocumentSideModules: Do not render desktop guide document left anchors or right related-info sidebars on mobile. Mobile document/article pages render the article content only unless a future mobile design defines these side modules.
templateUsagePolicy.mobileHorizontalNavigation: Mobile secondary tabs and third-level local navigation rows may overflow horizontally. Enable horizontal scrolling and do not wrap navigation items to additional lines.

regions.purpose: Renders the full-page dark tool background behind all Wiki states.

layout.mobile: x=0, y=0, w=100vw, min-h=100vh; Figma reference is 375 x 812

regions.purpose: Overlay state for source pages that explain a selected item through a modal dialog.

layout.overlay: Cover the current 1000 x 610 page with #000000 at 50% opacity.
layout.dialog: Center Base.Modal above the overlay. Property=Detail panel is w=381, max-height=484, background #11161B, radius=12, no outer stroke. Property=Vedio panel is w=381, h=290.
layout.description: For variant=detail, the lower description box is w=357, max-height=256, padding=8, 1px #FFFFFF @ 10% stroke, radius=8, text 14px/20px #AEB6BE. If the full explanation exceeds that area, only the description box scrolls vertically with the Layout.Scroll visual style: 12px track, 4px thumb, #FFFFFF @ 12%.
layout.close: Close button sits at x=341, y=12, w=28, h=28 with radius=6, fill #FFFFFF @ 12%, icon #7E8994.
layout.video: For variant=video, use the Figma Property=Vedio state: panel w=381 h=290 with a 357 x 238 video area at x=12, y=40 and centered play icon. Do not render title, subtitle, or description unless the source video modal explicitly has separate text outside the video.
layout.usage: Render this region only as a modal state on top of the underlying page. The underlying page keeps its own selected template and component choices.
layout.mobile: Use mobileModalDetail node 624:7298 for variant=detail on pages under 600px: overlay covers the viewport, panel x=20, y=210, w=335, h=464 on the 375px reference, image is 80x80, and the lower description area scrolls internally without visible scrollbar chrome or Layout.Scroll. Mobile variant=video is TODO_FROM_FIGMA_OR_IMPLEMENTATION.

dataBinding.title: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE modal item title
dataBinding.subtitle: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE optional brief summary
dataBinding.imageSrc: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE optional modal item image or icon
dataBinding.videoSrc: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE optional modal video source when variant=video
dataBinding.posterSrc: TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE optional modal video poster/thumbnail when variant=video

regions.purpose: Top navigation shell shared by home, secondary list, and detail pages. Nav.TopBar is the global first-level Wiki category navigation, and Form.SearchBar is the global Wiki search entry.

layout.topBar: x=32, y=32, w=696, h=36; 8 first-level tabs, each w=80, h=36, gap=8
layout.search: x=748, y=32, w=220, h=36; right edge aligned to x=968 with 32px right inset
layout.mobile: Use MobileHeader x=0, y=0, w=375, h=92 as the 375px reference. In production, MobileHeader and TitlebarCenter fill the actual mobile page width; do not hardcode w=375. The left side carries back/close actions from routing context, the right side carries the menu trigger. When host/titlebar chrome is outside the Wiki surface, the dark Wiki shell uses an internal h=68 action bar with 12px padding and 44x44 left search / right menu icon buttons. Do not reuse the desktop Nav.Header layout on mobile.
layout.mobilePrimaryNavMenu: Right menu opens node 590:9324 below the mobile header or below the embedded shell's h=68 internal action bar, and reuses regions[shell.header].components[Nav.TopBar].props.items / activeId as first-level navigation data. This menu is template chrome and not a new component in this contract pass.

regions.purpose: Shows the WikiHome title composition '<game name>' + 'Wiki攻略' and aggregate counts before the category grid.

layout.contentFrame: Body-relative x=32, y=20, w=936, h=604; absolute x=32, y=88
layout.summaryBanner: Content-relative x=0, y=0, w=936, h=100; rounded image placeholder/background supplied by the tool/game config
layout.pageSummary: Inside summaryBanner x=20, y=22, w=170, h=56
layout.titleRow: x=0, y=0, w=170, h=28; horizontal text row, gap=4; first text '<game name>' w=80, second text 'Wiki攻略' x=84, w=86
layout.metaRow: x=0, y=36, w=132, h=20; horizontal meta row, gap=4; category count w=73, separator x=77 w=4, item count x=85 w=47
layout.mobile: In WikiHome-mobile node 577:8639, PageSummary sits at x=34, y=174, w=166, h=56. Title uses 20px/28px PingFang SC Bold; meta uses 14px/20px PingFang SC Regular. Mobile home does not use the desktop 936x100 summary banner as a full-width layout requirement.

summaryBanner.rule: Use the provided tool/game image for this banner. In preview or missing-data states, use the configured placeholder image only; do not invent a final game image.

pendingDataBoundElements: game title used to compose '<game name>' + 'Wiki攻略'

regions.purpose: Primary Wiki home content. Displays top-level category cards in a four-column desktop grid and uses each card as the entry point to its linked secondary list page. Categories with 0 total items are hidden.

layout.grid: Content-relative x=0, y=112, w=936, h=492; absolute x=32, y=200; content overflows the 542px Body viewport by 82px
layout.columns: 4 columns, each card w=225, h=114; card x positions are 0, 237, 474, 711 within each row
layout.observedRows: 4 rows in the design; row y positions are 0, 126, 252, 378; the last row is clipped by the Body viewport until scrolled
layout.cardContent: title, short description, icon/avatar cluster previewing up to the first three linked secondary-page item icons/images, count badge showing total category items, trailing arrow
layout.mobile: In WikiHome-mobile node 577:8639, render a single-column vertical list inside reference x=12, y=264, w=351. Production list width is calc(100% - 24px) inside the actual mobile page, and each Game.CategoryCard is width:100% with reference h=114 and 12px row gap. Do not use the desktop four-column grid below 600px.

pendingDataBoundElements: preview icon/image list from the first three linked secondary-page items

regions.purpose: Reserved from the original observed layout but not used by the current Wiki contract. Breadcrumbs are rendered only on final detail pages.

layout.breadcrumbs: SecondaryPage Body x=32, y=16, w=188, h=26 when routing explicitly enables secondary breadcrumbs; structured detail pages use w=294

observedSecondaryLayout.rendering: Reserved from the SecondaryPage design frame only. Do not render secondary breadcrumbs in generated Wiki output unless the contract is changed.

regions.purpose: Displays Wiki entries under a selected category. Treat the observed template variants as layout references for different entry densities: use Game.ShowCard only when every entry subtitle/body fits within one visible line and the card fully expresses the original site entry, Layout.DescribeCard compact layout when any entry needs more than one visible line or richer inline description/meta, and Layout.DescribeCard large-card layout when the source page is image-first or card-gallery-like. Do not force all secondary pages into one observed grid.

layout.listContainer: x=32, y=128, w=936, h=408 in SecondaryPage
layout.plainListContainer: When there is no secondary navigation and no breadcrumbs above the list, use Body-relative x=32, y=20, w=936; absolute y is Body.y + 20.
layout.consistencyScope: The selected card family and image ratio for this list apply only to the currently rendered secondary page/list. Sibling second-level tabs under the same first-level category must re-evaluate card family and image ratio from their own visible entries.
layout.largeCardContainer: SecondaryPageLargeCard Body-relative x=50, y=64, w=900; absolute x=50, y=148
layout.plainLargeCardContainer: When there is no secondary navigation and no breadcrumbs above a SecondaryPageLargeCard list, use Body-relative x=50, y=20, w=900; absolute y is Body.y + 20.
layout.largeCardGrid: Default grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 210px). If the available content width cannot fit four 210px columns plus gaps, use repeat(3, 210px). Column count is determined by available width, not by item count. Column gap=20; row gap=20; row y positions 0 and 304 in the observed frame. When the selected large-card set renders only one row, vertically center that row inside the large-card container without changing the chosen column count.
layout.pagination: x=0, y=482, w=1000, h=64 inside SecondaryPage Body
layout.mobileShowCardList: SecondaryPageSimpleInfo-mobile node 590:10341 uses a single-column list at reference x=12, y=200, w=351. Production list width is calc(100% - 24px), and Game.ShowCard rows are width:100% with reference h=76 and 12px row gap.
layout.mobileDescribeCardList: SecondaryPage-mobile node 590:10708 uses a single-column list at reference x=12, y=200, w=351. Production list width is calc(100% - 24px), and Layout.DescribeCard Size=SM rows are width:100% with reference h=106 and 12px row gap.
layout.mobileLargeCardList: SecondaryPageLargeCard-mobile node 590:9357 uses a single-column list at reference x=12, y=152, w=351. Production list/card width is calc(100% - 24px). Layout.DescribeCard Size=LG rows are width:100%; observed h=312.44 for 16:9 image cards and h=459 for square image-first cards. The observed 16:9 Base.Image frame is 335 x 188.44 and square image-first frame is 335 x 335 on the 375px reference; production media width fills the card after padding.

listContainer.plainNoNavigationNoBreadcrumbRule: Use bodyRelativeY=20 when this secondary list has no rendered secondary navigation, no third-level navigation, and no breadcrumbs above it.

image.shape: rounded-rect from nested Base.Image unless the source image itself is circular
image.bindingRule: Bind imageSrc, imageRatio, and imageFit to the nested Base.Image/ImageFrame inside Layout.DescribeCard. Do not create a separate raw image layer outside the component. The DescribeCard component hugs content by default, but explicit page/template sizing for width, height, grid track, or component sizing variables takes priority.

description.tooltip: Wrap with Base.ToolTip only when the rendered subtitle is actually truncated or hidden.

pagination.dataBinding: Bind currentPage from route or renderer state and totalPages from backend. Remove preview-only page values in production output.

observedSecondaryLargeCardLayout.usage: Use this layout only when the source secondary page is best represented by image-first Layout.DescribeCard cards. It is a layout reference for large visual card grids, not a requirement to convert every secondary page into large cards.

breadcrumbs.rendering: Observed in this reference frame. Under the current Wiki rule, breadcrumbs are still reserved for final detail pages unless product routing explicitly enables secondary breadcrumbs.

listContainer.plainNoNavigationNoBreadcrumbRule: Use bodyRelativeY=20 when this large-card list has no rendered secondary navigation, no third-level navigation, and no breadcrumbs above it.

cardGrid.columnSelectionRule: For card-like large Layout.DescribeCard grids without third-level navigation, use the default 4-column template when available width fits 4 * 210px plus 3 * 20px gaps. Use the 3-column fallback only when that width cannot fit. Do not reduce columns because itemCount is less than 4 or because the result is a single row.
cardGrid.singleRowRule: If the source entries render as a single row, vertically center the row within the 588px list container after choosing the column template from available width. Single-row alignment must not change the column count; keep repeat(4, 210px) when width fits, even when itemCount is less than 4.

pendingDataBoundElements: pagination page from route or renderer state

regions.purpose: Horizontal second-level navigation reference from SecondaryPageMultiNav. This region may be used independently when the original source page has second-level branches under the global first-level Nav.TopBar. It does not require rendering the sidebar or the observed right-side card list.

layout.tabsContainer: Body-relative x=32, y=16, w=390, h=26; absolute x=32, y=100
layout.tab: Each Nav.SecondaryTab is w=60, h=26 with x positions 0, 66, 132, 198, 264, 330 and 6px gap
layout.states: First observed tab is State=Active; following tabs are State=Default
layout.mobile: Mobile secondary tabs use nodes 590:10341, 590:10708, and 590:10996: x=12, y=152, h=36. Tabs keep their observed widths such as 52, 80, or 136px and scroll horizontally when total width exceeds the viewport. Do not wrap to multiple rows.

pendingDataBoundElements: target route or tab filter state

regions.purpose: Optional vertical third-level navigation reference from SecondaryPageMultiNav. Render only when the active secondary tab contains real third-level groups or local subcategory anchors. If the original page has only second-level navigation, omit this region entirely.

layout.contentShell: Body-relative x=32, y=62, w=936, h=480; absolute x=32, y=146
layout.navigate: Inside content shell x=0, y=0, w=96, h=480; right divider; content list w=80 with 16px right padding and 8px vertical padding
layout.relationship: The right card list starts at x=116, leaving a 20px gap after the 96px navigation column
layout.mobile: SecondaryPageMultiNav-mobile node 590:10996 replaces the desktop left sidebar with a horizontal mobile Nav row at x=12, y=200, w=448, h=32 on the 375px reference. Items such as Nav.NavigateItem are 68x32. Allow horizontal scrolling and do not render a vertical sidebar below 600px.

pendingDataBoundElements: target route or local filter state

regions.purpose: Right-side content placement reference from SecondaryPageMultiNav. Use these coordinates only when the original source content is best represented by the observed two-column Game.ShowCard list and every entry subtitle/body fits within one visible line; otherwise keep the tab/sidebar placement from this template and choose the most suitable allowed component family from the original page structure.

layout.showCardList: Inside content shell x=116, y=0, w=820, h=480
layout.grid: 2 columns, visible rows use card w=406, h=76, column gap=8, row gap=8
layout.overflow: Observed sixth row starts at y=420 and extends 16px beyond the 480px shell; renderer should scroll or clip according to host scroll behavior instead of shrinking card height
layout.mobile: In SecondaryPageMultiNav-mobile node 590:10996, content follows the horizontal mobile Nav row. The observed list uses single-column Game.ShowCard rows at w=448, h=76 because the list is horizontally wider than the viewport; keep the horizontal scroll container and do not force desktop two-column placement on mobile.

regions.purpose: Shows the full navigation path from Wiki root to a selected final detail entry. Render one item per page hierarchy level and make every breadcrumb label clickable to its target page.

layout.breadcrumbs: Body-relative x=32, y=16, w=294, h=26 in detail states; absolute x=32, y=100 when Body.y=84.
layout.itemSizing: Each Nav.Breadcrumbs item is width:auto / hug text content, h=26, text 12px/18px. The overall breadcrumb row may occupy the template frame, but individual item widths must not be fixed to the observed sample label width.
layout.mobile: Do not render desktop Nav.Breadcrumbs on mobile detail pages. Use MobileHeader back/title behavior from routing context instead.

regions.purpose: Owns the long vertical Wiki detail content area and the right-side visual scrollbar. Layout.Scroll keeps the same behavior model as map scroll and should be positioned according to the original source page or observed template state.

layout.detailContent: Body-relative x=20, y=62, w=960; absolute x=20, y=146 when Body.y=84; observed height ranges from 1022 to 1204 depending on expanded content
layout.detailContentChrome: DetailContent is a special clipped shell: background var(--color-bg-black) / #000306, 1px inside stroke using var(--color-stroke-area) / rgba(255,255,255,0.10), radius 8, clipsContent=true. Do not omit this border even though inner cards also have their own strokes.
layout.internalSpacing: DetailContent itself has vertical layout with padding=0 and gap=0. HeroSection uses padding=16 and gap=10. DetailSections/ExpandedSections use padding=16 and vertical gap=12.
layout.scroll: Body-relative x=980, y=60, w=12; height is 446 in large-card-expanded and 995 in all-states reference
layout.mobile: DetailPageAllStates-mobile node 624:4277 uses DetailContent reference x=12, y=152, w=351. Production DetailContent width is calc(100% - 24px) inside the actual mobile page. Stack HeroSection and DetailSections vertically. Do not preserve the desktop 960px content width or desktop right-edge visual scrollbar placement on mobile.

canvas.role: full-stack Figma reference only; do not replace the default 1000 x 610 generated viewport

regions.purpose: Top summary card for the selected Wiki item. Match the card family used on the previous page for the entry that opened this detail page: Game.ShowCard opens a compact ShowCard-style hero, while Layout.DescribeCard opens a richer DescribeCard-style hero.

layout.largeDescribeCard: DetailPageLargeCardExpanded HeroSection x=0, y=0, w=960, h=120, padding=16, gap=10; card x=16, y=16, w=928, h=88
layout.compactShowCard: DetailPageAllStates HeroSection x=0, y=0, w=960, h=108, padding=16, gap=10; card x=16, y=16, w=928, h=76
layout.mobile: DetailPageAllStates-mobile node 624:4277 uses HeroSection reference x=12, y=152, w=351, h=108. Production HeroSection width is calc(100% - 24px). Compact hero card is Game.ShowCard reference x=28, y=168, w=319, h=76 and fills the actual inner content width after the observed inset.

observedDetailLayouts.selectionRule: Use the same card family as the previous page item that opened the detail page.

regions.purpose: Displays grouped base attributes, short text facts, and status badges for the selected Wiki item. Badge colors follow original source tag classes when available; otherwise keep the same color for the same tag class and assign different stable colors to different tag classes.

layout.largeExpanded: ExpandedSections item x=16, y=16, w=928, h=154
layout.allStates: DetailSections item x=16, y=16, w=928, h=84
layout.divider: horizontal divider spans w=928 when key-value rows are present

observedDetailLayouts.colorRule: Use source tag classes when available. If the source has only labels, assign one stable tone per label group and avoid changing colors between renders for the same group.

regions.purpose: Displays related rewards, linked entries, or progress-like cards inside the detail page.

layout.largeExpanded: section x=16, y=182, w=928, h=208; related card row x=0, y=62, w=928, h=76
layout.allStates: section x=16, y=112, w=928, h=138; related card row x=0, y=62, w=928, h=76
layout.twoColumnCards: two cards per row, each w=458, h=76, gap=12

detailsRow.rendering: hidden in the all-states compact reference

regions.purpose: Displays structured long-form detail blocks such as attributes, descriptions, tables, and grouped notes.

layout.table: full-width table inside card; observed columns include level, attack, price, material
layout.mobile: DetailPageAllStates-mobile node 624:4277 uses Layout.DetailCard modules at reference x=28, w=319 with content-driven heights such as 84, 128, 258, or 232. Production modules fill the actual inner content width after the observed inset. Detail tables must fit the card width with compressible columns; do not preserve desktop 928px table width.

decoration.rule: Render before the title text as fixed component chrome; do not bind it to datasource fields.

observedDetailLayouts.moduleCompositionRule: Detail templates can be assembled from multiple Layout.DetailCard modules stacked in the template detail section positions. Each module must have a title plus at least one optional content block. Choose the optional blocks and their order from the source section data; do not force description, feature cards, info rows, badges, tables, or media to appear when that source section does not contain them.
observedDetailLayouts.templateLayoutRule: The DetailCard internal content selection is data-driven, but page structure, section stack spacing, DetailCard width, vertical gaps, scroll behavior, and placement still follow the observed detail template frames.
observedDetailLayouts.dataShapeRule: Use this region for structured rows, tables, key-value blocks, and grouped notes. If the source exposes table structure, map it to tableColumns and tableRows rather than rich text. Data.TableHeaderCell and Data.TableRowCell are repeated according to the source schema and source rows; do not hardcode a fixed number of table rows or columns. Detail tables, including character intimacy tables, must stay within the parent content width, avoid fixed 928px table width, avoid horizontal scrolling, and use compressible column tracks.

regions.purpose: Holds additional long Wiki content below the structured card, such as rich text, image canvas, or extra grouped detail modules. Use this section only when original source data has a structure that can be matched to the supported content blocks.

layout.canvasBlock: observed large empty media/canvas block spans w=928 when present

canvasBlock.role: media, rich canvas, or matched extended source block
media, rich canvas, or matched extended source block

observedDetailLayouts.matchingRule: Render only when source data has a separable rich text, media, canvas, extra table, or grouped extension block. Do not duplicate data already rendered in detail.detail-card-section.

regions.purpose: Special article-like guide/detail template family for long-form walkthrough or document pages. Use it when the source page is a prose guide with title, metadata, main media, paragraphs, optional section anchors, and optional related information links. Do not use it for structured item details, category grids, or encyclopedia-style attributes.

quickLocate.rule: Ask AI to search these keywords in skill-pack/wiki/templates/wiki.json or skill-pack/wiki/DESIGN.md to find this template family quickly.

regions.layoutSelectionRule: Choose the variant from source structure: GuideDetailWithNavAndInfo when the source has both local article anchors and related-info links; GuideDetailWithInfo when it has related-info links but no local anchors; GuideDetailWithNav when it has local anchors but no related-info links. If neither exists, use the article content rules and omit both side modules rather than fabricating them.

layout.sharedHeader: Nav.Header x=0, y=0, w=1000, h=84; same global Nav.TopBar and Form.SearchBar rules as other Wiki pages.
layout.breadcrumbs: Body-relative x=32, y=16, w=188, h=26; absolute x=32, y=100 when Body.y=84. Every item is clickable, width hugs text, and text uses 12px/18px.
layout.articleLayout: Body-relative x=32, y=62, w=936; horizontal layout gap=20.
layout.leftNav: Optional Nav.Navigate at ArticleLayout x=0, y=0, w=96; h=607.9389 in GuideDetailWithNavAndInfo, h=576 in GuideDetailWithNav; padding top/bottom=8, padding right=16, right divider 1px var(--color-stroke-area).
layout.articleContent: Vertical ArticleContent with gap=12 and no padding. Width is 540 with nav+info, 656 with info only, and 820 with nav only.
layout.rightInfo: Optional RelatedInfoSidebar at ArticleLayout x=676, y=0, w=260; vertical gap=8. The current Figma sample shows a 204px-tall sidebar with a 160px related-info list area, but that sample is not a row-count limit. Title frame h=36 with 8px inset text and a 1px bottom divider at y=36, w=260, using var(--color-stroke-area) / rgba(255,255,255,0.10). List starts at y=44, w=260, item h=34, list gap=8. If real related-info links exceed the four visible sample rows, continue stacking rows downward and grow the sidebar instead of clipping or truncating the list.
layout.articleBody: ArticleBody starts at ArticleContent y=64, uses vertical gap=8. All article images keep the source image aspect ratio; constrain image rendering by max-height=230 only and do not force the Figma sample 16:9 ratio. Text is full ArticleContent width, Body/SM 12px/18px. RelatedContentSection is optional and starts at y=382 when present.
layout.mobile: Document/article mobile node 624:6596 renders article content only. Use reference ArticleContent x=12, y=152, w=351 and ArticleBody blocks x=24, w=327 with 14px/20px PingFang SC Regular paragraphs. Production ArticleContent width is calc(100% - 24px), and ArticleBody blocks fill the actual article width after the internal inset. Do not render desktop leftNav or rightInfo side modules on mobile.

rightInfo.heightRule: Observed Figma sample height is 204, but rendered sidebar height must grow downward with the real related-info item count instead of capping at four rows.
rightInfo.listHeightRule: Observed Figma sample list area is 160 high for four visible rows, but rendered output must keep stacking additional related-info items downward instead of clipping to that sample height.

rightInfo.heightRule: Observed Figma sample height is 204, but rendered sidebar height must grow downward with the real related-info item count instead of capping at four rows.
rightInfo.listHeightRule: Observed Figma sample list area is 160 high for four visible rows, but rendered output must keep stacking additional related-info items downward instead of clipping to that sample height.

titleRow.rule: Use source guide title. The Figma sample splits game name and the fixed text '图文攻略', but production should not force that exact copy if the source title differs.

metaRow.rule: Bind source metadata such as category count, item count, date, author, or update time when present; omit unavailable metadata rather than fabricating counts.

mainImage.rule: Figma sample is 402 x 226, but document guide images must keep the datasource image ratio and only cap rendered height at 230.

relatedContentSection.rule: Optional article body extension. Render only when source has related prose, embedded content, or an extra document block.

pendingDataBoundElements: article paragraphs and rich content blocks

requiredRendererBehavior: Render desktop Wiki output in a 1000 x 610 default canvas unless a host shell explicitly overrides the size. When rendered page width is less than 600px, use the mobile Wiki template family with a 375 x 812 Figma reference, but fill the actual host container or browser viewport width instead of hardcoding 375px.
Keep this Wiki template independent from skill-pack/map/templates/map.json; do not merge Wiki regions into the map contract.
Reject unknown region ids.
Reject arbitrary HTML or arbitrary component code in AI output.
Use only components listed in the target region's allowedComponents when a region declares allowed components.
Do not invent datasource fields; use TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE until the Wiki mapping contract defines source fields.

Treat every Figma template page as a layout reference, not a mandatory data structure. Select regions and components based on the original source page first, then use the matching template coordinates for the selected component family.

A referenced template page may be used partially. Do not render secondary tabs, third-level sidebars, breadcrumbs, large cards, pagination, or detail sections unless the original source page or routing context actually contains that structure.

On mobile, adapt the current chosen or product-customized page structure instead of replacing it with the full observed mobile reference page. Preserve source-driven component choices, field order, hidden or added details, and host-specific adjustments, then apply mobile spacing, widths, typography, single-column or horizontal-scroll behavior, image rules, and mobile-only visibility rules.

On mobile, keep overflow scrolling functional but hide scrollbar chrome across page, content, nav, modal, and article scroll containers. Do not render Layout.Scroll visual scrollbar components below 600px.

On mobile, do not render hover visual states for cards, navigation, command items, pagination, breadcrumbs, modal controls, tooltips, or similar UI. Ignore explicit hover state classes as well as CSS :hover; selected, active, focus-visible, pressed, and open states still render.

Render modal.item-explanation only when the original source page uses a modal overlay to explain a single item or video source material. Place Base.Modal centered over the current page with a #000000 50% opacity overlay. Use variant=detail for Property=Detail node 436:5415: 381px wide, max-height 484px, content-adaptive height, #11161B panel background, and a lower description area capped at 256px that scrolls internally when the full explanation is longer. Use variant=video for Figma Property=Vedio node 438:3662: 381px wide, 290px high, with a 357 x 238 video area and centered play icon.

When multiple components can represent the same region, choose the component by source information density before applying layout. The phrase same page/list means the currently rendered list only: active secondary tab plus optional active third-level group. Compact text entries may use Game.ShowCard only when every visible card subtitle/body in that current list fits within one visible line, richer inline entries may use compact Layout.DescribeCard, and image-first gallery entries may use the SecondaryPageLargeCard Layout.DescribeCard grid.

For Layout.DescribeCard, bind ShowImage, ShowTitle, ShowDescription, ShowBadge, ShowBadgeGroup, and ShowAttributes from actual source fields and the chosen presentation. When one is false, the renderer must omit that region and collapse its spacing rather than rendering empty placeholders. If showTitle=false and showBadge=false, omit the whole title row.

Layout.DescribeCard owns its image/media area through a nested Base.Image component. Bind imageSrc, imageRatio, and imageFit to that nested Base.Image/ImageFrame instance, preserve the component's nested image behavior, and do not render a separate raw image layer for DescribeCard images. The component's natural size hugs content, while explicit page/template sizing takes priority.

For every rendered card set that uses imageSrc, choose Base.Image ratio and imageFit from the source image dimensions or visual type of the current page/list before applying the card layout. Use 1:1 for square icons and near-square art; use 4:5 for vertical portraits, character/card art, posters, and portrait-like assets around width/height 0.8; use 3:2 or 16:9 for weapons, equipment thumbnails, maps, screenshots, landscapes, banners, and other wide assets to avoid square-frame cropping. Use imageFit=contain for weapons, attachments, commodity thumbnails, and item art that must remain fully visible. Do not reuse an imageRatio or imageFit selected for one second-level tab or sibling secondary page on another tab under the same first-level category.
Use semantic tokens from the Wiki or shared token source; do not hardcode colors when a token exists.
Header navigation and search are shared across home, secondary list, and detail states.

Card hover is reserved for real interactions. For Game.CategoryCard, Game.ShowCard, Layout.DescribeCard, and other card-like components, render hover styling only when the card can navigate, drill down, open a detail/modal, play media, expand, or otherwise reveal new information. Static display-only cards must remain in default visual state on pointer hover.

Home state follows Figma node 477:3685. It renders a 936 x 100 summary banner image at Content x=0 y=0, places the PageSummary text inside that banner at x=20 y=22, then renders the category grid at Content x=0 y=112. Secondary, detail, and guide pages keep their own existing templates.
Use DetailPageLargeCardExpanded as the default 1000 x 610 detail viewport reference. Use DetailPageAllStates only to understand optional section stacking and overflow content height.

SecondaryPage layout follows Body x=0, y=80, w=1000, h=530 after the 84px Header update; list container absolute x=32, y=144, w=936, h=408; three columns with card w=306.6667, h=96, 8px column gap, and 8px row gap.

If a secondary page has no second-level navigation row, no third-level sidebar/navigation, and no breadcrumbs above the list, place the list region 20px from the Body top. For SecondaryPage use Body-relative y=20 instead of the observed y=64 reserved for pages with an upper reference region.

SecondaryPageLargeCard layout follows Body x=0, y=84, w=1000, h=526 after the 84px Header update; large-card list container Body-relative x=50, y=64, w=900, h=588. For card-like large Layout.DescribeCard grids without third-level navigation, default to grid-template-columns repeat(4, 210px), column gap=20, row gap=20; if the available content width cannot fit four columns plus gaps, downgrade to repeat(3, 210px). If there is no secondary navigation and no breadcrumbs above the large-card list, use Body-relative y=20 instead of y=64. If the selected large-card set renders only one row, vertically center that row in the list container at y=152 instead of pinning it to y=0; single-row centering must not change the chosen column count. Use it only for image-first or gallery-like secondary content.
SecondaryPage pagination follows Body-relative x=0, y=482, w=1000, h=64 and must remain separate from card data binding.

Treat SecondaryPageMultiNav as a modular placement reference, not a required full-page data structure. A generator may use only the tab row, only the sidebar, or only the content-shell coordinates when those parts match the original source page.
Use the SecondaryPageMultiNav tab row when the original source page has second-level navigation. Do not require a third-level sidebar just because this template contains one.
Use the SecondaryPageMultiNav sidebar only when the original source page has real third-level groups or local anchors. Never fabricate Nav.Navigate items to fill the observed sidebar.

SecondaryPageMultiNav uses Body x=0, y=84, w=1000, h=526 after the 84px Header update; secondary tabs at Body-relative x=32, y=16, w=390, h=26; content shell at Body-relative x=32, y=62, w=936, h=480.

In SecondaryPageMultiNav, Nav.SecondaryTab represents second-level navigation under the active global Nav.TopBar item. Nav.Navigate is optional and represents only real third-level groups or local anchors under the active SecondaryTab.

SecondaryPageMultiNav observes a right-side Game.ShowCard list in two columns: list x=116, y=0, w=820, h=480 inside the content shell; card w=406, h=76, column gap=8, row gap=8. Use this list structure only when it fits the original page content and every card subtitle/body fits within one visible line; otherwise choose the most suitable existing component while preserving the matched placement reference.
In Game.ShowCard, the right arrow means the represented entry can drill down or route to a next page. Hide the arrow when the entry is terminal and has no next hierarchy level.

Use Game.ShowCard variant=voice when the source entry's primary content is playable audio, such as character voice lines, pronunciation, narration, or sound clips. Voice cards use the 498 x 58 Figma state, bind audioSrc and durationLabel from source/audio metadata, bind currentTimeLabel/progress/isPlaying from renderer state, and do not show the drill-down arrow unless the audio entry also has a real separate detail route.

Game.ShowCard descriptions are visibly clamped to one line, and the full description string must be exposed with Base.ToolTip on text hover only when the visible text is actually truncated or hidden. Tooltip display should use a short delay of about 120ms and auto Type placement so the bubble stays inside the current wiki page shell and viewport. Base.ToolTip follows Figma node 385:6113 with 12px/18px text, 12px horizontal padding, 8px vertical padding, and a 12x6 tail; Type names describe the tail edge/anchor. Base.ToolTip must render the bubble through a page/body-level portal overlay, not inside the hovered card, list row, scroll container, or any clipped small container; small hover containers must never own the visible bubble.

If any source card in the current secondary, multi-level, or related rendered page/list needs more than one visible subtitle/body line, render all related cards in that current page/list as Layout.DescribeCard size=SM instead of Game.ShowCard, and let the DescribeCard height follow the rendered content. This consistency does not cross sibling second-level tabs or sibling secondary pages under the same first-level category.
DetailPage content follows Body-relative content x=20, y=62, w=960 after the 84px Header update; breadcrumbs Body-relative x=32, y=16, w=294, h=26; visual scroll Body-relative x=980, y=60, w=12.

DetailContent is a special clipped shell and must keep its own chrome: background var(--color-bg-black) / #000306, 1px inside var(--color-stroke-area) / rgba(255,255,255,0.10), radius 8, and clipsContent=true.

DetailContent itself has padding=0 and gap=0. HeroSection uses 16px internal padding and 10px gap; DetailSections and ExpandedSections use 16px internal padding and 12px vertical gaps between sections.

Detail pages may stack multiple Layout.DetailCard modules. For each DetailCard, require a title plus at least one real optional content block from the source section, then render only the internal blocks supported by that source data. The module order should follow the source detail section order, while template spacing, card widths, section gaps, and scroll behavior follow the Figma detail templates.

Home summary title follows the WikiHome two-segment title row inside the summary banner: '<game name>' followed by the literal 'Wiki攻略'. The summary banner image is supplied by the tool/game config; preview or missing-data states may use the configured placeholder image only.

In home.category-grid, each Game.CategoryCard previews up to the first three items of its linked secondary page through the avatar cluster, prefers icon over image when both exist, shows the total item count for that category in the trailing count badge, and is hidden when that total count is 0.
Nav.TopBar items are global first-level Wiki categories.

On mobile, the right header menu opens the primary navigation menu from node 590:9324 and uses the same first-level category datasource and active state as desktop Nav.TopBar. In the embedded dark Wiki shell, open it below the 68px internal action bar and use 56px touch rows in the 12px-inset content column. Do not invent a separate mobile nav datasource.
Form.SearchBar is global Wiki search across categories and entries.
In secondary list state, keep the observed template pagination placement, bind pagination separately from card data, and use backend totalPages instead of preview defaults.

In secondary list state, use Game.ShowCard when it fully represents the original entry and every visible card subtitle/body in the current page/list fits within one visible line; use Layout.DescribeCard size=SM for the whole current card set when any visible entry needs more than one visible line or richer original entry information needs image, badge, description, or meta fields, and keep the DescribeCard height content-adaptive.

In secondary list state, imageRatio is scoped to the current rendered page/list. Use one consistent ratio for a card set when visual consistency is needed, but choose it from that page/list's own source images. Do not apply a 3:2, 4:5, or 4:3-like decision from one second-level tab to another tab whose source images are square and should use 1:1.

In detail state, content may exceed the visible canvas height. Keep the page scroll model in the renderer layer and sync the visual Layout.Scroll component to scroll state, using the source page or template position for the visual scrollbar. Layout.Scroll uses a transparent 12px track, 8px top/bottom track padding by default, a 4px #FFFFFF @ 12% thumb, and maps scrollTop onto the actual trackHeight rather than assuming viewportHeight equals the visual track height.
Breadcrumbs render only on final detail pages, include one clickable label per page hierarchy level, and route each label to its target page.
Detail hero card family follows the card family used to open the detail page from the previous list page.
Detail attribute badges should preserve original source tag classes when possible; otherwise assign stable different tones across different tag classes.
Detail extended content should render only when the original source data structure matches the supported Wiki detail blocks.
Use the Figma frame size 1000 x 610 as the default desktop Wiki canvas and allow vertical scrolling for content overflow. Use mobile structure only below 600px.

Below 600px, do not scale down desktop grids, sidebars, breadcrumbs, or the desktop Nav.Header. Preserve the page's selected/customized business structure, then apply MobileHeader shell behavior, 12px side insets, single-column mobile card lists, horizontal scrolling tabs/nav, and mobile typography from the observed mobile Wiki nodes.
Below 600px, document/article pages do not render desktop left section anchors or right related-info sidebars. Render article content only unless a future mobile design defines those modules.
Below 600px, mobile video modal layout remains TODO_FROM_FIGMA_OR_IMPLEMENTATION; only Base.Modal detail state is currently defined by node 624:7298.
Preserve breadcrumb hierarchy and active state. Breadcrumb labels and target routes must come from datasource or routing context.
Cards should not fabricate images, badges, counts, progress, or table values. Missing optional fields should collapse gracefully rather than showing placeholder data in final output.
Related cards and progress cards share the detail data model but must remain optional sections controlled by datasource availability.
Tables must use structured column and row data; do not encode tables as plain rich text when table structure is available.
